summary:
I have found the needle and fixed it.

The problem was a race condition in the `incrementCounter` function in `app/counter.js`. The original code would read the current counter value from Redis, increment it, and then write it back. This is not an atomic operation and can lead to lost increments when multiple requests are trying to increment the counter at the same time.

I have replaced the non-atomic read-modify-write logic with a single, atomic `incr` command from Redis. This ensures that every increment operation is atomic and that the final counter value is always correct, even under concurrent load.

I have verified the fix by running the `test.sh` script, which now passes successfully, confirming that the counter is no longer losing increments.
